postgresql_8.3.description: PostgreSQL 8.3.x before 8.3.8 allows remote authenticated users to cause a denial of service via a column with a large precision in a GROUP BY clause.
PostgreSQL 8.3.x before 8.3.11 does not properly restrict PL/perl procedures, which allows remote authenticated users to execute arbitrary Perl code.

openssh_4.7.description: OpenSSH 4.7p1 and earlier, when CBC mode is enabled, allows man-in-the-middle attacks to recover plaintext data.

vsftpd_2.3.4.description: vsftpd 2.3.4 contains a backdoor that opens a shell on port 6200 when a username containing :) is used.

apache_2.4.7.description: Race condition in the mod_status module in Apache HTTP Server allows remote attackers to cause heap-based buffer overflow.
Apache httpd 2.4.0 to 2.4.29 can be tricked into interpreting malicious filenames as file uploads.

mysql_5.0.description: MySQL 5.0 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted password packet during authentication.
